creator: areilly@bigpond.net.au
text:
A while ago I had a 300G Maxtor firewire external hard
        drive, and it worked perfectly for years until it died
        horribly a month ago.  I replaced it with a WD My Book
        1028 firewire drive, and the system has been unable to
        boot properly since.  Often it would not even get to
        single-user mode, waiting for xpt_config, as has been
        previously reported.  I'm pleased to report that today's
        build seems to have fixed that problem, at least for me.
        The Firewire drive is still not recognized though, so
        the system falls into single-user mode, because the FW
        drive is listed in my /etc/fstab.

        While in single-user mode, if I unplug the FW drive and
        then plug it back in, the device is scanned properly and
        appears as /dev/da1 as intended.  I can then mount -a
        and ctl-D to continue the boot and everything is nice.

        I *could* work around the boot issue by taking the drive
        out of /etc/fstab, but I would still have to do the
        manual reinsertion dance before the drive showed up in
        /dev and was mountable, and that's not good.

Fix: 

Manually connecting the drive *after* the system is
        booted (at least to single-user shell prompt) works, but
        isn't a fix.

How-To-Repeat:         Not sure how someone who doesn't have this hardware
        could repeat the problem, but it does this every time,
        for me.  I'm happy to run tests and patches if anyone
        wants me to.
